title: Dysphonia follow-up

presenting_history.text: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.
presenting_history.rationale: The exact dysphonia history section supports interval voice change, onset, functional impact, associated features, relevant negatives, and clinician-performed ENT or neck assessment without diagnosing the cause.
presenting_history.evidence_extract: Defines dysphonia and hoarseness, describes voice-quality changes, common causes, vocal demand, tobacco exposure, reflux context, and prevention topics.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

red_flags.text: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.
red_flags.rationale: The exact escalation section supports clinician concern and review of already available specialist information without generating laryngoscopy or referral.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Requires history and examination for modifying factors and expedited laryngeal evaluation for neck mass, respiratory distress or stridor, tobacco history, recent procedures, and persistent symptoms.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

exact_section.heading: Key action statements: assessment, escalation, and laryngoscopy

medication.text: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.
medication.rationale: The exact outcomes section supports concerns, clinician-entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending voice therapy, medicine, or surgery.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented preventive discussion, questions, follow-up, and change in voice or quality of life.

source.title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Hoarseness (Dysphonia) (Update) – official fact sheet and key action statements

exact_section.heading: Key action statements: education and outcomes

source_limitations: Voice quality and duration, occupation, smoking, intubation, surgery, reflux and neurological context, laryngeal examination, diagnosis, treatment, therapy, referral, and outcome remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
